 {"id":"brenner_bot-ysc","title":"Task: Implement swipe navigation between documents","description":"Add gesture-based navigation between documents on mobile.

## Purpose

Swipe navigation is a natural mobile pattern. Users expect to swipe left/right to go between items.

## Scope

- Swipe LEFT to go to NEXT document
- Swipe RIGHT to go to PREVIOUS document
- Only on document pages (/corpus/[doc])
- Threshold: 50% of screen width or 100px, whichever is less

## Technical Approach

### Option A: CSS Scroll Snap
- Wrap prev/current/next in scroll container
- Use scroll-snap-type for snapping
- Detect scroll end for navigation

### Option B: Touch Event Handling
- Listen for touchstart/touchmove/touchend
- Calculate swipe distance and velocity
- Trigger navigation if threshold met

### Option C: Library (react-swipeable)
- Use existing gesture library
- Less code, more reliable
- Small bundle impact (~3KB)

Recommended: Option C for reliability, unless bundle size is critical.

## Visual Feedback

- Show edge peek of prev/next during swipe
- Elastic bounce at first/last document
- Cancel swipe if released before threshold

## Accessibility

- Swipe is enhancement, not requirement
- Prev/Next buttons always visible
- Works without gesture support

## Files to Modify

- Create gesture wrapper component
- Wrap document page content
- Handle navigation events

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Swipe left/right navigates documents
- [ ] Visual feedback during swipe
- [ ] Threshold prevents accidental navigation
- [ ] Edge behavior at first/last doc
- [ ] Doesn't interfere with content scrolling
- [ ] Prev/Next buttons still work","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:13.330351376-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented swipe navigation on /corpus/[doc]","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ysc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:24.946600519-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ywhq","title":"TASK: Web App ↔ Agent Mail Integration Bridge","description":"
## The Non-MCP Bridge

### The Problem
Per AGENTS.md:
- Coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) use MCP tools NATIVELY
- The web app CANNOT use MCP (runs in browser/server)
- The web app needs `apps/web/src/lib/agentMail.ts` HTTP client

But how exactly does the web app:
1. Dispatch agents to do work?
2. Receive agent responses?
3. Monitor agent status?

### Architecture

#### 1. Agent Dispatch Pattern
The web app doesn't call agents directly. Instead:

```typescript
// User clicks "Get Devil's Advocate Analysis"
async function requestAgentAnalysis(
  sessionId: string,
  agentType: 'devils_advocate' | 'experiment_designer' | 'brenner_channeler',
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard
): Promise<void> {
  // 1. Create a thread in Agent Mail
  const thread = await agentMailClient.createThread({
    subject: `Analysis Request: ${agentType}`,
    metadata: { sessionId, agentType, hypothesisId: hypothesis.id }
  });
  
  // 2. Post request message to the thread
  await agentMailClient.sendMessage({
    threadId: thread.id,
    to: ['AgentCoordinator'], // A daemon that dispatches to actual agents
    subject: `Please analyze hypothesis`,
    body: formatHypothesisForAgent(hypothesis),
  });
  
  // 3. Store pending request in session
  await updateSession(sessionId, {
    pendingAgentRequests: [...session.pendingAgentRequests, {
      threadId: thread.id,
      agentType,
      requestedAt: new Date(),
    }]
  });
}
```

#### 2. Agent Coordinator Daemon
A background process (NOT the web app) monitors Agent Mail and dispatches to actual agents:

```typescript
// Runs as a separate process (brenner.ts or dedicated script)
async function agentCoordinatorLoop() {
  while (true) {
    const inbox = await agentMailClient.fetchInbox('AgentCoordinator');
    
    for (const message of inbox.filter(m => !m.acknowledged)) {
      const { agentType, hypothesisId } = message.metadata;
      
      // Spawn appropriate agent (in a tmux pane via ntm)
      await spawnAgent(agentType, message.threadId, hypothesisId);
      
      await agentMailClient.acknowledgeMessage(message.id);
    }
    
    await sleep(5000);
  }
}
```

#### 3. Response Flow
When agent completes, it posts to the thread. Web app polls or uses SSE to detect:

```typescript
// Real-time hook checks for completed analyses
async function checkCompletedAnalyses(sessionId: string) {
  const session = await getSession(sessionId);
  
  for (const pending of session.pendingAgentRequests) {
    const thread = await agentMailClient.getThread(pending.threadId);
    const agentResponse = thread.messages.find(m => 
      m.from !== 'AgentCoordinator' && m.from !== 'WebApp'
    );
    
    if (agentResponse) {
      // Agent has responded!
      await handleAgentResponse(sessionId, pending.agentType, agentResponse);
      await removePendingRequest(sessionId, pending.threadId);
    }
  }
}
```

### Key Components

#### agentMail.ts Enhancement
Current file needs:
- Thread management
- Message metadata support
- Inbox filtering
- Acknowledgement tracking

#### Agent Coordinator
New daemon that bridges web requests to actual agents:
- Monitor incoming requests
- Spawn appropriate agents
- Track agent status
- Handle timeouts

#### Session State
Track pending agent requests:
```typescript
interface SessionState {
  // ... existing fields
  pendingAgentRequests: PendingAgentRequest[];
  completedAnalyses: CompletedAnalysis[];
}
```

### File Locations
- `apps/web/src/lib/agentMail.ts` - HTTP client (enhance)
- `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/agentDispatch.ts` - Dispatch logic
- `brenner.ts` or `scripts/agent-coordinator.ts` - Coordinator daemon

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Web app can request agent analysis
- [ ] Coordinator receives and processes requests
- [ ] Agent responses flow back to web app
- [ ] UI shows pending/completed status

### Depends On
- 1v26.1 (Session Data Model - needs pendingAgentRequests)
- xlk2.1 (Agent Roles - defines which agents exist)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-yz5","title":"Create Bayesian mapping visualization","description":"# Task

Build an interactive visualization of the implicit Bayesianism table from the README.

# The Table

| Brenner Move | Bayesian Operation |
|--------------|-------------------|
| Enumerate 3+ models | Maintain explicit prior distribution |
| Hunt paradoxes | Find high-probability contradictions |
| Third alternative | Reserve probability mass for misspecification |
| Design forbidden patterns | Maximize expected information gain |
| Seven-cycle log paper | Choose experiments with extreme likelihood ratios |
| ... | ... |

# Visualization Options

1. **Interactive table** - Hover for explanations, click for examples
2. **Animated flow** - Show the Brenner move flowing to Bayesian concept
3. **Card pairs** - Brenner move card links to Bayesian card

# The Objective Function

Display prominently:
```
                Expected Information Gain × Downstream Leverage
Score(E) = ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
              Time × Cost × Ambiguity × Infrastructure-Dependence
```

With explanation of what Brenner optimized (small denominator, large numerator).

# Design

- Clean table with subtle row hover
- Brenner moves in one color, Bayesian in another
- Expandable rows for full explanations
- Links to transcript quotes where applicable

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Table renders all 12 mappings
- [ ] Interactive hover/expand
- [ ] Objective function displayed
- [ ] Links to sources
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:05.719598117-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:45:51.050959453-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:45:51.050959453-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented interactive Bayesian crosswalk with 12 mappings, objective function display, and transcript anchor links","labels":["method","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.053470213-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-twy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:08.431879875-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"brenner_bot-z2kc","title":"Fix SessionDashboard shortcuts: guard shift/composition","description":"# Fix SessionDashboard shortcuts: guard shift/composition

## Problem
Global shortcuts in `SessionDashboard` were too eager:
- `Shift+ArrowLeft/Right` could advance phases (undesirable; shift-arrow is often used for selection/navigation).
- IME composition key events should be ignored.

## Fix
- Ignore `event.isComposing`.
- Require `!shiftKey` for non-modifier shortcuts (phase nav + single-letter actions).
- Add regression test for Shift+Arrow.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-z9y","title":"Task: Document mobile UI issues","description":"Review screenshots and catalog all mobile UI issues.

## Purpose

Systematic documentation of problems before fixing. This prevents ad-hoc fixes that miss issues.

## Issue Categories to Look For

### Layout Issues
- Content overflow (horizontal scroll)
- Improper stacking
- Elements too close to edges
- Safe area violations (notch, home indicator)

### Spacing Issues
- Inconsistent padding/margins
- Elements too crowded
- Breathing room for touch targets

### Typography Issues
- Text too small to read
- Lines too long (> 65 characters)
- Truncation problems
- Poor hierarchy

### Interactive Element Issues
- Touch targets < 44px
- Elements too close together
- Hidden/inaccessible controls
- Unclear tap states

### Visual Issues
- Alignment problems
- Inconsistent styling
- Dark mode contrast issues
- Animation/transition problems

## Deliverable

Markdown document with:
- Screenshot reference
- Issue description
- Severity (P0 blocking, P1 high, P2 medium, P3 low)
- Suggested fix

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All screenshots reviewed
- [ ] Issues categorized by type
- [ ] Severity assigned to each
- [ ] Document committed to repo (e.g., docs/mobile-audit.md)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:59.387282661-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:13:29.405100227-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:13:29.405100227-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile audit document complete at apps/web/docs/mobile-audit-2025-12-30.md. All pages screenshotted (30+), issues categorized with severity ratings, and document committed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-z9y","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:36.551894446-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-z9y","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-95h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:36.647631924-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-zhz5","title":"CLI compile: lint -> recommended operators + next actions","description":"# Goal\nMake brenner session compile guide the next move: map lint violations to recommended Brenner operators and concrete next actions.\n\n# Deliverables\n- Implement mapping from key lint IDs (EH-003, WT-001, EA-002, WP-001, etc.) to operator recommendations.\n- Print recommendations in human mode after lint report.\n- Include recommendations in JSON output payload.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:25:58.080731932-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:51:04.658358438-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:51:04.658358438-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-zhz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-l9ce","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:25:58.084591112-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-zmc3","title":"Task: ntm Template Library for Brenner Prompts","description":"# Goal
Create .ntm/templates/ with Brenner-specific prompt templates for operator use.

# Why 5 Templates (Restored)
They're just text files. Role-specific templates are useful for targeted requests without cluttering the general templates.

# The Templates

## 1. brenner_join_session.md
For agents just starting - orient them to the session.
```markdown
# Session: {{session}}

You are joining a Brenner Loop research session.

## Setup
1. Check Agent Mail inbox for thread: {{session}}
2. Read the KICKOFF message to understand the research question
3. Note your assigned role (if role-specific session)

## Your Role
Check the thread for role assignment. Common roles:
- **Hypothesis Generator**: Propose hypotheses including third alternative
- **Test Designer**: Design discriminative tests with potency checks
- **Adversarial Critic**: Critique the framing, find real third alternatives

## Response Format
Reply with structured DELTA blocks per specs/delta_output_format_v0.1.md

## Ground Rules
- Cite transcript anchors (§n) or label claims as [inference]
- Include rationale for each delta
- Check Agent Mail after posting for feedback
```

## 2. brenner_request_delta.md
General request for structured contribution.
```markdown
# DELTA Request: {{session}}

Please contribute to thread {{session}} with structured DELTA blocks.

## Requirements
1. Use fenced ```delta code blocks
2. Follow specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md  
3. Ground claims in transcript anchors (§n) or label [inference]
4. Include rationale for each delta

## What to Contribute
Based on your role and the current artifact state, add:
- Hypotheses (including third alternative)
- Predictions for each hypothesis
- Discriminative tests with potency checks
- Assumptions with load/test analysis
- Critiques of the framing

## Check Before Submitting
- [ ] Each delta has operation, section, payload, rationale
- [ ] Claims are grounded or labeled [inference]
- [ ] Response addresses gaps in current artifact
```

## 3. brenner_discriminative_tests.md
Role-specific: Test Designer focus.
```markdown
# Discriminative Test Request: {{session}}

Design discriminative tests for thread {{session}}.

## Test Quality Criteria
1. **Likelihood ratio**: Aim for 10x+ separation between hypotheses
2. **Potency checks**: Include chastity (if test passes, is it meaningful?) and impotence (if test fails, is it informative?)
3. **Expected outcomes**: Specify what each hypothesis predicts
4. **Feasibility**: Note cost, time, and resource requirements

## Output Format
```delta
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Test name",
    "procedure": "Step-by-step procedure",
    "discriminates": "H1 vs H2",
    "expected_outcomes": {
      "H1": "What H1 predicts",
      "H2": "What H2 predicts"
    },
    "potency_check": "How we know the test is meaningful",
    "feasibility": "Cost/time estimate"
  },
  "rationale": "Why this test discriminates"
}
```

## Reference
- §50: Brenner on discriminative tests
- §103: Third alternative thinking
- specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md
```

## 4. brenner_adversarial_critique.md
Role-specific: Adversarial Critic focus.
```markdown
# Adversarial Critique Request: {{session}}

Provide adversarial critique for thread {{session}}.

## What to Attack
1. **Framing assumptions**: What if the question itself is wrong?
2. **Hidden third alternatives**: What are we missing?
3. **Metric confounds**: Are we measuring the right thing?
4. **Exception quarantine**: Are anomalies being swept under Occam's broom?

## Output Format
```delta
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "adversarial_critique",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Critique name",
    "attack": "What's wrong with the framing",
    "evidence": "Why this attack has teeth",
    "current_status": "Active/needs investigation",
    "real_third_alternative": true  // If this is the REAL alternative we're missing
  },
  "rationale": "Why this critique matters"
}
```

## The Real Third Alternative
At least one critique should be marked `real_third_alternative: true`. This isn't just "both hypotheses could be partly right" - it's a genuinely different explanation that reframes the problem.

## Reference
- §103-105: Brenner on third alternatives
- §160: Exception quarantine
```

## 5. brenner_round_complete.md
Signal end of contribution round.
```markdown
# Round Complete: {{session}}

This round of {{session}} is complete. Compilation pending.

## What Happens Next
1. Operator runs `./brenner.ts session compile`
2. Artifact is updated with all DELTAs
3. COMPILED message posted to thread
4. Critique round may follow

## What to Do Now
- Review any pending ack_required messages
- Acknowledge with `mail ack`
- Wait for COMPILED artifact before sending more DELTAs

## If You Have Late Contributions
Hold them. They'll be included in the next round after compilation.
```

# Implementation

## Directory Structure
```
.ntm/
  templates/
    brenner_join_session.md
    brenner_request_delta.md
    brenner_discriminative_tests.md
    brenner_adversarial_critique.md
    brenner_round_complete.md
```

## Usage
```bash
# General requests
ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_join_session --all
ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_request_delta --all

# Role-specific requests
ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_discriminative_tests --pane 2
ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_adversarial_critique --pane 3

# End of round
ntm send "$THREAD_ID" --template brenner_round_complete --all
```

## Variable Substitution
- `{{session}}` - ntm substitutes with session name
- Future: `{{role}}`, `{{target_repo}}`, `{{artifact_url}}`

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] .ntm/templates/ directory created
- [ ] All 5 templates implemented
- [ ] Templates reference correct spec sections
- [ ] {{session}} substitutes correctly
- [ ] Usage documented in cockpit runbook

# Relationship to Other Work
- Complements: One-command cockpit runner (other agent)
- References: specs/delta_output_format_v0.1.md, specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:51:46.820630462-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:37:58.87425679-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T19:10:30.192871341-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: .ntm/config.toml and .ntm/templates/* exist with Brenner templates; issue is redundant.","labels":["infrastructure","ntm","templates"]}
